reasoning: 2023 set a historic global temperature record, and 2024 is on track to be similarly warm or warmer due to the delayed heating effects of a strong El Niño that peaked in early 2024. ENSO forecasts predict a shift toward La Niña or ENSO-neutral conditions prevailing through late 2024 and into 2025. La Niña typically leads to cooler global average surface temperatures, offsetting the underlying greenhouse warming trend by around 0.1–0.2 °C compared to El Niño years. Given this expected cyclical cooling, it is highly unlikely that 2025 will surpass the records set during the 2023/2024 El Niño phase. A probability of 2% aligns well with climatological base rates and the current ENSO trajectory.
